tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubinuria), suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease. Metabolic acidosis is indicated by low CO₂ and high chloride. Glycosuria with low HbA1c suggests possible renal glycosuria rather than diabetes. Urine findings may also indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further workup.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
